title: Experiencing singularity in the now HIP Theory
display_title: Experiencing singularity in the now HIP Theory
wikitext:
[]: Experiencing-singularity in the now. (HIP Theory)             Quote Post by Ritsuko » Thu Feb 24, 2011 4:41 pm Wouldn't being one lifeform be similar to as we are now except for the infinite bliss part? Think of it like this, we humans feel our angry side, sad side , when we were young, as teens and so forth, but we never perceive those emotions or past selves as different beings. When I look back at my actions in life I feel like I'm not the young me neither the teenage me, yet they both feel like 2 different parts of me instead of actually being me. Truth is, I am all those past emotions and events forming the present me. Now if we times this by 6 billion I would feel like one heck of a ultimate being. Being all and every person has one flaw and that's the nature desire to grow/learn/evolve. Even if Shinji did accept HIP I believe down the road this new oneness-being would try to create a universe or reality within itself creating infinite amount of lifeforms in order to learn more. Godlike being experiencing the infinite by muiltiplying ones self within. The beings on all planets wouldn't be aware they're apart of god (until ready) for it would only damage the learning process.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Feb 24, 2011 4:43 pm have you taken your pills today, Ritsuko?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Feb 24, 2011 5:08 pm I'm not really sure what you're on about. Can you explain it a bit clearer and in more detail?

[]: Re: Experiencing-singularity in the now. (HIP Theory)             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Feb 24, 2011 5:13 pm Ritsuko wrote: Wouldn't being one lifeform be similar to as we are now except for the infinite bliss part?

[]:             Quote Post by Ritsuko » Thu Feb 24, 2011 5:38 pm Guys lol Ok, What I mean is like how do I know I'm not many selves already. Just what the hell are we humans. Am I the body? The brain or the thoughts within my brain? Just how can we be human if we're in our brains as shapeless, formless thought awareness. How do we know we are single beings if we're just thought/awareness?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Feb 24, 2011 5:39 pm oh great, it's one of these threads... here we go now

[]:             Quote Post by Ritsuko » Fri Feb 25, 2011 7:55 am Xard wrote: oh great, it's one of these threads... here we go now

[]:             Quote Post by Aozora » Fri Feb 25, 2011 11:25 am Read up on some philosophy? Or maybe you just have...

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Feb 25, 2011 1:51 pm Aozora wrote: Read up on some philosophy?

[]:             Quote Post by Ritsuko » Fri Feb 25, 2011 2:26 pm Allemann wrote: THAT he did not do. I assure you.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Feb 25, 2011 3:48 pm Marijuana is awesome, isn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Feb 25, 2011 4:13 pm So we are not, this body we "are in" but the whole world and everyone is essentially just a persona of mine? Sounds plausbile enough, but why is it in the Evangelion Discussion sub-forum?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Feb 25, 2011 5:12 pm Ritsuko wrote: We = Not the body We = Not the brain We = Are the awareness inside this state of existense. With that side it means we have no gender, singularity, or real body. I think I'm one person because I'm only aware of one of my many selves.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Fri Feb 25, 2011 5:22 pm With that side it means we have no gender, singularity, or real body. I think I'm one person because I'm only aware of one of my many selves.

[]:             Quote Post by Seen » Fri Feb 25, 2011 5:39 pm Are you trying to say that we could all be gods as a result from instrumentality that forgot about our pasts over time, and we became lonely so we made offspring? Is this what you are going at? Anywhere close? I'm kinda scared if it is.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Feb 25, 2011 5:58 pm Ritsuko wrote: Nobody wanna challenge Ritsuko's logic? I'll try it again. We = Not the body We = Not the brain We = Are the awareness inside this state of existense. With that side it means we have no gender, singularity, or real body. I think I'm one person because I'm only aware of one of my many selves.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Feb 25, 2011 6:09 pm TehDonutKing wrote: The idea is nothing new, as NemZ said. It is in pretty much every religion, usually referred to as a "soul." You're probably familiar with it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Feb 25, 2011 7:17 pm TehDonutKing wrote: "soul."

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Feb 25, 2011 7:27 pm NemZ wrote: I don't know if there's any particular reason such a pattern/system can't exist in a different physical substrate though, if that's what you're on about.

[]:             Quote Post by Ritsuko » Fri Feb 25, 2011 9:42 pm NemZ wrote: I'm still not getting your point, if there is one. "You" are just the self-referential aspect of an electrochemical process in a particular state that occurs within the brain (and by extension, the body) at some specific point in time. I don't know if there's any particular reason such a pattern/system can't exist in a different physical substrate though, if that's what you're on about. That said, if the pattern contains principles relating to a particular gender or body wouldn't they still in a sense posses them? Why couldn't a purely digital "you" still consider yourself as having physical qualities even if they aren't (or possibly never were) true? Viewing yourself as the sum of all "you"-states isn't a particularly new idea. Besides, the distortions of memory mean that an individual person can probably never have a true understanding of themselves in such a view, nor can anyone else.
